text: Muschelli J (2026). actiquantiles: Map Activity Data Normalized Quantiles. R package version 0.0.1, https://github.com/jhuwit/actiquantiles.
code: @Manual{,   title = {actiquantiles: Map Activity Data to Normalized Quantiles},   author = {John Muschelli},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.1},   url = {https://github.com/jhuwit/actiquantiles}, }

what: Map Activity Data to Normalized Quantiles
title: Map Activity Data to Normalized Quantiles
text: actiquantiles maps physical activity values NHANES-based quantiles. package exposes acti_-prefixed wrapper around mapnhanespa::map_nhanes_pa_quantiles() public API can stay stable new mapping backends added later. Core entry points: acti_map_nhanes() participant-level quantile mapping mapnhanespa::nhanes_pa_quantile() single value lookup mapnhanespa::nhanes_pa_age_category() age binning

title: Map Activity Data to Normalized Quantiles
text: can install actiquantiles GitHub : package depends mapnhanespa, provides NHANES mapping tables lookup logic.

title: Map Activity Data to Normalized Quantiles
text: wrapper keeps input columns adds acti_pa_quantile: single value, use scalar helper mapnhanespa directly:
code: example_data <- data.frame(   id = c("A", "B"),   age = c(25, 62),   sex = c("Female", "Male"),   measure = c("mims", "ssl_steps"),   value = c(15000, 7500) )  mapped <- acti_map_nhanes(example_data) mapped #>   id age    sex   measure value acti_pa_quantile #> 1  A  25 Female      mims 15000        0.5349443 #> 2  B  62   Male ssl_steps  7500        0.3527381 head(mapped) #>   id age    sex   measure value acti_pa_quantile #> 1  A  25 Female      mims 15000        0.5349443 #> 2  B  62   Male ssl_steps  7500        0.3527381 mapnhanespa::nhanes_pa_quantile(   value = 15000,   age = 25,   sex = "Female",   measure = "mims" ) #> [1] 0.5349443

what: Map physical activity values to NHANES population quantiles — acti_map_nhanes
title: Map physical activity values to NHANES population quantiles — acti_map_nhanes
text: map_nhanes_pa_quantiles() adds population-level quantile column participant-level data frame. Quantiles evaluated NHANES accelerometer cumulative distribution functions stratified age category, sex/gender, measure, optionally survey wave.

title: Map physical activity values to NHANES population quantiles — acti_map_nhanes
code: acti_map_nhanes(   data,   id = NULL,   age = "age",   sex = "sex",   measure = "measure",   value = "value",   wave = NULL,   age_category = NULL,   quantile_col = "acti_pa_quantile" )

title: Map physical activity values to NHANES population quantiles — acti_map_nhanes
text: data data frame one row per participant-measure observation. id Optional participant identifier column name. column checked supplied, otherwise left unchanged. age, sex, measure, value Column names data containing age years, sex/gender, physical activity measure, observed value. Set age = NULL use age-overall CDFs. Set sex = NULL use sex/gender-overall CDFs. Setting NULL uses overall CDF across dimensions. wave Optional NHANES wave column name scalar value. Supported values 7, 8, "2011-2012", "2013-2014". NULL, combined wave CDFs used. age_category Optional column name containing NHANES age categories "[20,30)" "Overall". supplied, used instead age. quantile_col Name output quantile column.

title: Map physical activity values to NHANES population quantiles — acti_map_nhanes
Map physical activity values to NHANES population quantiles — acti_map_nhanes
code: example_data <- data.frame(   id = 1:2,   age = c(25, 62),   sex = c("Female", "Male"),   measure = c("mims", "ssl_steps"),   value = c(15000, 7500) )  acti_map_nhanes(example_data) #>   id age    sex   measure value acti_pa_quantile #> 1  1  25 Female      mims 15000        0.5349443 #> 2  2  62   Male ssl_steps  7500        0.3527381
